summary: Created github_fetch.py module that fetches GitHub profile data via REST (no auth) and GraphQL (with token), outputs structured JSON with profile metrics, languages, top repos, contribution calendar, and streaks.
decisions: Used stdlib only (urllib, json) to avoid external dependencies as specified
Implemented graceful degradation: REST-only mode when no token, full data with token
Handle 404 by returning response body instead of raising exception to match spec error format
Aggregate languages by repo size (bytes) not count for more accurate weighting
Calculate streaks from contribution calendar with current streak tracking from today/yesterday backwards
Output matches exact JSON schema from brief including warnings array and error handling
learnings: GitHub GraphQL requires bearer token, REST works without auth but has lower rate limits
Rate limit retry_after calculation needs to handle X-RateLimit-Reset header which is epoch timestamp
Streak calculation is complex: current streak requires checking today/yesterday as starting point then counting backwards
ISO week format (YYYY-W##) requires isocalendar() week number extraction
Sparse profile warning threshold of <5 repos helps identify candidates with private repos
